title: get_blocking scoped-thread pattern: must not be replaced with block_in_place or Handle::block_on
description: get_blocking() uses std::thread::scope to spawn a thread with its own tokio runtime when called from inside an existing runtime. The comment explicitly rules out tokio::task::block_in_place (panics on current_thread runtime, the default in #[tokio::test]) and Handle::block_on (same reason). The scoped-thread pattern works in all runtime configurations because the new thread never interacts with the caller's runtime. This pattern is correctly replicated in all three AWS backend constructors (aws-sm, aws-ssm, aws-kms build_client functions). A contributor might simplify these to block_in_place, which would break all tests and single-threaded service deployments.
